summary.rationale: Negative employee sentiment; moderate innovation; 40 reviews

reviews.title: Not bad
reviews.pros: Part time is a good thing
reviews.cons: Low hours / wages, too much drama often

reviews.title: They don't pay enough for the work you do.
reviews.pros: The remote days we used to have. The people are nice and if you can put up with staying longer theres is a good chance of moving up from within. Good representation among employee groups.
reviews.cons: They will take away benifits while telling you employees asked for it. I had two remote days a week when I started. They took away one last year and this year they took the other. You dont get company match for 401k investments until a year in. Instead of meaninful raises you will be advertised to by companies who give out free samples in the lobby and this will be presented as a perk. You will not be trained and will have to learn how to do the job on your own.
reviews.advice: Stop forcing social events, pay better for the amount of work you ask for out of employees. There needs to be some sort of training documentation or program, most knowledge leaves with former employees or is taken with employees when they are promoted.

reviews.title: Heading downhill
reviews.pros: Mostly this is an easy going environment.  There is stress, but not excessive and most upper management (director level) care about their teams.
reviews.cons: Senior management is out of touch with the people that work in the store support center and probably the stores too.  They decided to have a return to work from a hybrid system, but left one day at home.  Now they are taking that away too.  They claim we asked for it, but that's all obvious lie.  If it were true the change would be unnecessary since everyone would just come in.  It's crazy how 90% of my meetings are on teams and not in person.  It's just stupid management that will hurt DG in the long run.  Back out to the job market I guess.
reviews.advice: Be visible.  I rarely see VPs and they require us to be in the office when many aren't there themselves.  Go back to hybrid working conditions.

reviews.title: Working for DG
reviews.pros: It wasnt the best job honestly but it was a good place to work if you like a fast paced environment. My boss was absolutely wonderful and she did the best she could with minimal power and control over staffing on a local store level
reviews.cons: It was horrible in terms of staffing  they could have done so much better instead of leaving to a high stress low empathy environment where we were bombarded by customers constantly and had to manage customer intake along with stocking

reviews.title: The store manager is trying but is limited by higher-ups
reviews.pros: Flexible scheduling for those who study or have other duties
reviews.cons:
Poor organization, management trying to push you harder and work faster despite knowing their employees have debilitating injuries. 

Expect to work with 1 other person at most during your shift.

Upper management urging store manager to fire low productivity workers while they're already struggling to hire, yet refuses to pay people higher wages. 

The back storage area is filled to the brim, you seriously cannot get through that area. The inventory ordering system seems to be at least partially automated and keeps sending the same items even though they never sell - it's very flawed and the place is not run well. While I was there, it seemed that the manager was at least trying, but upper management does not give many resources
reviews.advice: For upper management, please pay staff better, both the store associates and the store manager, they deserve higher wages for what they're going through working here. Better pay and better working conditions. Give the store higher budgets to allow for more employees to work simultaneously so the back warehouse area can also be sorted out.

reviews.title: Lots of room for improvement
reviews.pros:
Some flexibility 
Your team
You do have some say on your store
reviews.cons: Pay, micro management, lack of decent management above you, they give you no hours to effectively run your store, solo shifts, a lot of hours you work
reviews.advice: If you hire quality store managers. Give them the tools and leeway to manage that store. Your company would prosper and your customers will thank you. There is much more to be said but this is the shortest and simplest.

reviews.title: Lack of understanding and support from management
reviews.pros:
Stocking work was straightforward and enjoyable.
First manager was supportive, respectful, and gave positive feedback.
Job can be manageable with proper expectations and structure.
Coworkers were generally fine to work with.
reviews.cons:
Management after the first manager left was unsupportive and dismissive.
Constantly expected to do multiple people’s jobs at once.
No consideration for employees who work best focusing on one task at a time.
Communication issues and lack of appreciation for good work.
When I explained that I am autistic and have anxiety, accommodations or understanding were not provided.
Repeatedly pushed beyond reasonable limits, which created unnecessary stress.
reviews.advice: Listen to your employees and recognize individual strengths. Not everyone works the same way, and productivity improves when people are assigned tasks they do well instead of being overloaded. Be more understanding of mental health and neurodiversity, and don’t ignore employees who are genuinely trying and putting in effort. Appreciation and basic respect go a long way toward retaining good workers.

reviews.title: Customer Service Professional
reviews.pros: Leadership; advancement, associate degree in criminology; civil law, currently, obtaining bachelor’s degree in psychology with an LPN license private and family primary caregivers travel!
reviews.cons:
Consider to advance, more knowledge, updated technology techniques, creative credentials for career development and opportunities!
NONE
reviews.advice: LEADERSHIP; ACCOUNTABILITY, RELIABILITY, ALUMNI, LEADERSHIP PROFESSIONAL!

reviews.title: It was alright
reviews.pros: I got paid weekly and Sometimes I would get an hour break
reviews.cons:
My main keyholder/person who would lock up the store and take care of most things would constantly sit in the break room and make me do all the work
There was only one register
Management was very lackluster
reviews.advice: Be more attentive and make sure your workers are actually doing there jobs

reviews.title: Management
reviews.pros: Associates are friendly and helpful
reviews.cons: Management sucks and can't train properly
reviews.advice: Learn proper communication

reviews.title: It completely depends on your location and team
reviews.pros: -If you have a good team, dollar general can be a fairly straightforward retail job that offers a broad range of skills needed for day-to-day operations in a relatively friendly work environment. I really liked my team and customers overall, and it offered me an opportunity to get started again after a gap in my resume with consideration for my health issues. They were extremely supportive and I was so thankful for the opportunity; but that only applies to this location, not the broader company.
reviews.cons:
-Since staffing is so light as a policy, not only do you juggle many hats at all times, but it can be a safety hazard. (A coworker may not hear you fall or a customer can easily corner you.)
-Female employees were made to feel unsafe by customers at times and coworkers had to watch each other's backs, as the manager would not ban them unless it happened in front of her. She was often also a victim of this behavior and was trying her best, it was a difficult situation all-around.
-DG Corp cares more for profits than employee safety and cameras are to catch employee theft, not monitor and document workplace safety/harassment.
-Shifts were often staffed by 1-3 employees max, with people often opening alone. Corporate limits of assignable hours were cited as a constant issue by management.
reviews.advice: -Make a formal method of complaint for inappropriate customer interactions to be documented and acted on so this isn't an ongoing issue. It emboldens these creeps when there's no consequence.

reviews.title: Run far away as quick as you can
reviews.pros: None that I can think of
reviews.cons: Extremely Inadequate training. Poor upper management. Endless work load. Inability to focus on your job as manager when you spend most of your time on a register during your shift. Always and I mean always short staffed.  Low pay. Massively high turnover and I see why. I could go on for days about the cons...
reviews.advice: Don't bother. You will burn out rapidly. They don't care about you.

reviews.title: Better Days
reviews.pros: The employees at this DG store are nice, friendly, and build relationships with the customers, bring them back everyday.
reviews.cons: While employees are stocking and straightening out the store, it's sometimes difficult to see when customers are ready for check out. They are focused on the task given to them and some customers understand, others are just rude about it.
reviews.advice: Keep doing a wonderful job satisfying each customers experience!!

reviews.title: Hardest you’ll ever work for the lowest you’ll ever be paid
reviews.pros: Fantastic job security because no one wants to work here, for many, many very good reasons.
reviews.cons: Almost everything else.  The company has zero consistency, and does not appear to be serious about improving anything at all.  At this point it’s more of a ponzi scheme.  Their same-store year-over-year growth is around 1%, because when you only build in small towns that do not grow in population, your stores’ customer base never increases, but to attract investment you must show growth.  This is why they keep building new stores.  So they need the investment to build stores, but they need to build stores to show growth to attract investment.  All this means is this company is a “growth” company and not a “retail” company, meaning they do not care about their stores at all beyond the first year, which means they do not care about their employees’ wellbeing at all, so much so that they treat employees as an expense they’d rather not have.
reviews.advice: Honestly, because it’s a “growth” company and not a “retail” company, keep doing what you’re doing.  It’s up to employees to figure out your game and decide whether or not they want to participate.

reviews.title: First time management only
reviews.pros: Make your own schedule.401k was good.
reviews.cons: You are the schedule. Expectations don't meet pay.
reviews.advice: Control work home life when building schedule this will take over if you are truly invested

reviews.title: Choose where you want to go each day and do .
reviews.pros: Work at you own pace.No one telling you how to do your job.
reviews.cons: 40 hrs a week no OT . Pay for mileage is too low. 8 hr work day .Hourly pay could be better.
reviews.advice: 8 Hr work day is not enough time to get anything done if driving  2 to 3 hrs one way .

reviews.title: Outdated review, but good experience
reviews.pros: Welcome and friendly staff, plenty of opportunities to grow.
reviews.cons:
Some unsavory customers, as is given in retail positions.
The crew I worked with has since left, but the new crew seems friendly too.
reviews.advice: Nothing to note, but it would be good to train new employees for Fedex packages if they don't already.

reviews.title: Meh job
reviews.pros: if you like meeting new people and making friends with regulars then this is something you will enjoy
reviews.cons: corporate will punish for almost anything and will over work you and barely be of help

reviews.title: Salary  in Dollar general
reviews.pros: great salary and work life balance
reviews.cons: no cons for this company

reviews.title: DG
reviews.pros: Good job to start with after High School, and while in college
reviews.cons: Depending on position you don’t really have any benefits that are beneficial to you as a worker.

reviews.title: Review
reviews.pros: Good hours for part-time position
reviews.cons: Too much for one person to do

reviews.title: Save yourself
reviews.pros: it's a job so there's that
reviews.cons: you do everything yourself, clean, run counter, stock shelves, price goods, receive goods

reviews.title: Starter Job
reviews.pros: Teaches you about customer service
reviews.cons: There’s little room for growth

reviews.title: Delivery driver
reviews.pros: The was beautiful when I first started making  1500 to 2000 a week take home
reviews.cons: I left because they've cut pay down to 1000 a week.
reviews.advice: This was a back breaking hard job and Dollar General should pay their drivers a higher income for all they do for this company. Pay these guys there due and stop playing with these guys money.

reviews.title: Entry level job
reviews.pros:
Flexible
Easy
Dibs on penny items
Most of the time customers where great.
reviews.cons:
Favoritism
Low pay
DM was rude
High turnover rate
Maintenance took forever to fix store issues.
reviews.advice: Regional and Distric Managers need to stick up for the Store managers more. Most of the time the store managers are having to pull teeth just to get anything done. When the store managers are in a bad mood the associates suffer a lot.

reviews.title: Could have been better...
reviews.pros: Acquired understanding of store operations such as inventory control, cash handling, and loss prevention. This varied greatly from experience in a big-box store, and it was an interesting change in work atmosphere.
reviews.cons:
The job fostered a culture where employees were expected to prioritize the company above all else, leaving little room for independence.

Compensation was limited to base pay, as opportunities for wage progression were not provided or explained.

Despite bringing significant years of retail experience, the role did not provide opportunities that reflected prior skills and contributions.

Management initially communicated that my role was being elevated; however, the upgrade did not occur, and further explanation was not given.

Company guidelines were not always fully adhered to at the location level, which affected workflow and expectations.

There were ongoing issues with morale at the store level, stemming from leadership and communication challenges.

There were situations where inaccurate assumptions led to misplaced accountability.
reviews.advice:
Acknowledge the skills and background employees bring with them. Providing fair opportunities for wage growth and advancement shows that experience is valued and encourages retention.

When discussing promotions, upgrades, or policy changes, clear communication and consistent follow-through build trust and credibility. Employees thrive when they understand the reasons behind decisions.

Ensuring that store policies are followed fairly and consistently helps create stability, reduces confusion, and builds employee confidence in leadership.

A positive work environment depends on open communication, respect, and recognition. Investing in team morale leads to higher motivation and better overall performance.

Demonstrating accountability, fairness, and professionalism sets the tone for the entire team and strengthens workplace culture.

reviews.title: Not Great
reviews.pros: What is expected of you is clearly outlined, no surprises or confusion.
reviews.cons: There's not enough time to get things done. You get management duties without management pay or benefits.
reviews.advice: Poverty wages will never inspire loyalty or hard work.

reviews.title: Store manager ruled the roost
reviews.pros: The customers are what kept me clocking in each day. The same faces became Mr or Mrs and helped to keep a friendly smile on my face
reviews.cons: Low, very low pay. Very limited hours per week. Very
reviews.advice: Put a smile on your face just as we do each shift. Delegate responsibility  to others so maybe u won't look, act, and show how run down you are and feel.

reviews.title: Dollar General Wage
reviews.pros: Not many to speak of. Pay is consistent I guess
reviews.cons: No raises. The amount of work you're responsible for there does not equal the compensation. Corporate couldn't care less about the store employees.
reviews.advice: Not much, no. Have some empathy for the workers

reviews.title: Over hire
reviews.pros: Laid back easy tasks and light cleaning
reviews.cons: No training or help next to no hours to give system crashes all the time rude coworkers

reviews.title: Over worked and under paid
reviews.pros: Pay is salary so your checks are consistent.
reviews.cons: You're required to work 48+hours, most of the time youre there 55 to 70. Since the SM is salary, you end up making less an hour than the associates.

reviews.title: Review
reviews.pros:
Good way to gain experience
Steady work
reviews.cons:
Hours get cut sometimes
Heavy work load

reviews.title: cashier
reviews.pros: chill, easy, funny, flexible, scenic
reviews.cons: pay, hours, certain customers, pay for parking

reviews.title: DOLLAR GENERAL
reviews.pros: Daily Pay, Work is easy.
reviews.cons: Demographic of customers leads to the workers being yelled at by strangers.
reviews.advice: Add Self Pay Lanes

reviews.title: Fine Stepping Stone
reviews.pros: Not many to note, it's a retail job.

reviews.title: Not enough pay
reviews.pros: It’s a job not a career

reviews.title: Its a job.

reviews.cons: inconsistent hours, making their "top performers" take the reins on all the things they cant be bothered to train the rest of the staff on. overworked and underpaid. favoritism in management.

reviews.title: Review
reviews.pros: Great company to work for
reviews.cons: Do not pay well at all

reviews.title: DG Review
reviews.pros: I had a great store manager.
reviews.cons: Skeleton staff. Low pay. Not enough help.
reviews.advice: Management for store did their best but company really fails in providing an enjoyable job environment.
